question.text: Why is independence important for the Compliance Officer in a securities market intermediary?

answer.text: To ensure that internal affairs are aligned with regulatory objectives rather than business expediency in case of conflict between the two
To enable the CO to run the business operations without board interference
To allow the CO to make investment decisions independently
To give the CO authority to override all management decisions

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The CO shall participate in preparing policies and procedures so that the internal affairs of the intermediary are aligned with the regulatory objective rather than business expediency in case of conflict. Independence frees the CO from elaborate internal reporting procedures and ensures minimal gap between regulatory intent and compliance. **The Trap:** Independence doesn't mean the CO runs the business or overrides management — it means the CO can report non-compliance directly to the BoD and SEBI without internal delays or pressure. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The dual reporting line (to BoD AND directly to SEBI) is the structural guarantee of CO independence. This prevents management from suppressing non-compliance reporting.
